message:
feat(workflows/runner): tick 178 run_workflow_with_cx entry-point shim (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

First tick of the workflows/runner.rs strangler-fig, deferred
since tick 131. Per tick 177's plan, this tick lands the cx-first
entry point as a pre-flight-only shim. Future ticks migrate the
300+ LOC body per-branch.

New sibling:
- WorkflowRunner::run_workflow_with_cx — pre-flight
  `cx.checkpoint()` gates the workflow start; on a cancelled
  caller cx, returns `WorkflowExecutionResult::Error` with
  `execution_id: Some(...)` and a "cancelled pre-start" error
  message before touching storage or policy. When cx is healthy,
  delegates to the legacy `run_workflow`.

Match-surface stability: uses the existing `Error` enum variant
(not a new `Failed`) so every `run_workflow` caller handles the
pre-cancel path without source changes.

Legacy `run_workflow` (L426) preserved unchanged. Its 2 call
sites at L1566 (inside `run`) and L1741 (inside
`resume_incomplete`) still route through the ambient path. Those
two callers don't have cx in scope today — migrating them to
`run_workflow_with_cx` is a future tick that needs to thread cx
through the enclosing fns first.

Next ticks in the strangler-fig:
- Migrate `run_workflow_with_cx` body: start action + storage
  reads in the startup branch (record_workflow_start_action,
  get_workflow, get_pane).
- Per-step loop: cx.checkpoint() at each step iteration.
- Storage calls inside step branches: route through _with_cx
  siblings.
- Eventually migrate the two legacy call sites in `run` +
  `resume_incomplete` to use run_workflow_with_cx.

Verified via fork bypass: 31/31 workflows::runner:: tests pass
(no regressions; new code compiles).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
